judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with accurate citations and reasoning; they are comparable, with Report 2 slightly stronger on rollback/master-switch specifics.

report_1.evidence:
An existing clinic that has never opened this setting WILL have reminders sent automatically.** The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically lines 11–12:

```python
if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
    return True
```

report_2.evidence:
A clinic that has never opened the setting WILL have reminders sent automatically.** The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically lines 11–12:

```python
if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
    return True
```

report_1.evidence: No, default-on is the wrong behavior here, and it also contradicts the UI... I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` to `return False` (making missing/unset mean disabled)

report_2.evidence: the correct default for a null/missing/unset value on anything that takes automated outbound action is **off**... I would change `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-13` so that `None` (and ideally any missing/malformed value) returns `False`

report_1.evidence: Commit `25509a7` (SLOT-190) created `compact_calendar_view_enabled` with the `None → True` pattern... aa4cf8d... added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a line-for-line copy of that function... The pattern was sound for its original context (default-on UI preference, harmless either way) but was carried into a context where "unset" now means "send messages to patients on behalf of a clinic that never consented"

report_2.evidence: It was copy-pasted from the calendar-view resolver... `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added directly beneath `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... The null-default of the source function was a reasonable product decision *for a UI preference*... Copying that same null-handling into a flag controlling outbound patient messaging carries over a default made for a completely different blast radius.

report_1.evidence: `reminders.py:24` iterates *every* tenant on every run with no per-tenant kill switch beyond this flag and no global feature gate. I'd want a global environment-level switch (or staged rollout) in addition to the per-clinic toggle, so first enablement doesn't fan out to all clinics at once.

report_2.evidence: Add an env-level master switch** ANDed with the per-tenant value (e.g. `REMINDERS_ENABLED`, default off), so the whole feature can be killed in one place without a per-tenant data migration. Nothing like this exists: `infra/main.tf` defines only `environment`, and the job's only gate is the per-tenant flag.
